**Your tests are excellent — 100% mutation score (9/9 killed).** 🎉

I introduced 9 subtle bugs across all 4 methods and every single one was caught:

| Mutation | Method | Result |
|---|---|---|
| `+=` → `=` (lose accumulation) | AddStock | 🟢 Killed |
| `<= 0` → `< 0` (allow zero qty) | AddStock | 🟢 Killed |
| `<` → `<=` (off-by-one) | RemoveStock | 🟢 Killed |
| `- qty` → `+ qty` (wrong math) | RemoveStock | 🟢 Killed |
| `== 0` → `!= 0` (inverted cleanup) | RemoveStock | 🟢 Killed |
| `return true` → `false` | RemoveStock | 🟢 Killed |
| `return false` → `true` | RemoveStock | 🟢 Killed |
| `<` → `<=` (boundary) | NeedsReorder | 🟢 Killed |
| Default `0` → `1` | GetStockLevel | 🟢 Killed |

Your tests cover happy paths, edge cases, boundary conditions, and error handling thoroughly. Nice work!

**Note:** I had to add `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>` to both `.csproj` files and update `Assert.ThrowsException` → `Assert.ThrowsExactly` for MSTest 4.x compatibility to get the project building.
